login.title: Sign in to your workspace
login.subtitle: Enter your credentials to continue.
login.saml_not_configured: SAML SSO is not configured yet. Please sign in with email and password.
login.setup_success_banner: Admin created. Sign in to continue.

error.oauth2_login_failed: We could not complete the sign-in with your provider. Please try again.
error.oauth2_email_missing: Your provider did not return an email address. Ask your administrator to grant the email scope.
error.oauth2_email_unverified: Your email address is not verified with the provider. Verify it and try again.
error.oauth2_local_email_conflict: An AccessFlow account with this email already uses a password. Ask an administrator to switch the account to single sign-on before retrying.
error.oauth2_unexpected_auth: Single sign-on returned an unexpected authentication state.
error.oauth2_unknown_provider: Single sign-on returned an unknown provider.
error.account_disabled: Your AccessFlow account is currently disabled.
error.generic: Single sign-on failed. Please try again.

setup.title: Create the first admin
setup.subtitle: No active admin user was found. Set up your organization and the first admin account to get started.
setup.footer: first-run setup · only available while no admin exists

validation.totp_code_pattern: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator.

oauth2.client_id_max: Client ID must be at most 512 characters.
oauth2.client_secret_max: Client secret must be at most 2048 characters.
oauth2.scopes_max: Scopes override must be at most 1024 characters.
oauth2.tenant_id_max: Tenant ID must be at most 255 characters.

errors.auth_generic: Sign in failed. Please try again.
errors.setup_already_complete: Setup is already complete — please sign in.
errors.email_already_exists: A user with that email already exists.
errors.setup_generic: Could not complete setup. Please try again.
errors.server_unreachable: Could not reach the AccessFlow server. Please retry.
errors.review_self_forbidden: You cannot review a query you submitted yourself.
errors.reviewer_not_eligible: You are not an approver at this stage.
errors.review_query_not_pending: This query is no longer pending review.
errors.review_query_not_found: This query is no longer available.
errors.review_generic: Could not record the review decision. Please try again.
errors.review_plan_in_use: This review plan is attached to a datasource and cannot be deleted.
errors.review_plan_name_already_exists: A review plan with that name already exists. Pick a different name.
errors.illegal_review_plan: That review plan configuration is not allowed.
errors.review_plan_generic: Could not save the review plan. Please try again.
errors.datasource_name_already_exists: A datasource with that name already exists in your organization. Pick a different name.
errors.ai_config_name_already_exists: An AI configuration with that name already exists. Pick a different name.
errors.ai_config_in_use: This AI configuration is bound to one or more datasources and cannot be deleted.
errors.illegal_user_operation_admin: This user change is not permitted (admins cannot demote or deactivate themselves).
errors.notification_channel_config_invalid: Channel configuration is missing required fields.
errors.notification_delivery_failed: Test notification could not be delivered.
errors.bad_audit_query: Audit log filter is invalid (page size or resource type).
errors.datasource_permission_already_exists: This user already has a permission row for this datasource.
errors.illegal_datasource_permission: That user is not part of your organization.
errors.datasource_grant_generic: Failed to grant access. Please try again.

driver_unavailable.offline_cache_miss: The JDBC driver for {{dbType}} is not in the local cache and AccessFlow is in offline mode. Pre-populate the driver cache or set ACCESSFLOW_DRIVERS_OFFLINE=false and restart AccessFlow. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.cache_not_writable: AccessFlow cannot write to the JDBC driver cache directory. Set ACCESSFLOW_DRIVER_CACHE to a writable path (for example, ~/.accessflow/drivers) and restart AccessFlow. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.download_failed: AccessFlow could not download the {{dbType}} JDBC driver from Maven Central. Check network connectivity, or set ACCESSFLOW_DRIVERS_REPOSITORY_URL to your internal Maven mirror and retry. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.checksum_mismatch: The downloaded {{dbType}} JDBC driver failed integrity verification. Delete the cached file and retry; if the problem persists, the configured Maven mirror may be tampering with the JAR. ({{detail}})
driver_unavailable.unavailable: The {{dbType}} JDBC driver cannot be loaded. Ask your AccessFlow administrator to verify the driver cache and registry. ({{detail}})

errors.admin_generic: Could not complete the request. Please try again.
errors.language_not_in_allowed_list: That language is not in the organization's allowed languages.
errors.totp_required: Enter the code from your authenticator app to continue.
errors.totp_invalid: That verification code is not valid. Please try again.
errors.totp_invalid_code: That verification code is not valid.
errors.password_change_not_allowed: Password change is not allowed for this account.
errors.profile_generic: Could not update your profile. Please try again.
errors.custom_driver_duplicate: A JDBC driver with this SHA-256 is already registered for your organization.
errors.custom_driver_in_use_one: Cannot delete: {{count}} datasource still uses this driver. Delete that datasource first.
errors.custom_driver_in_use_other: Cannot delete: {{count}} datasources still use this driver. Delete those datasources first.
errors.custom_driver_checksum_mismatch: The uploaded JAR's SHA-256 does not match the value you entered. Recompute the checksum and try again.
errors.custom_driver_invalid_jar: The uploaded JAR does not contain {{driverClass}}, or that class does not implement java.sql.Driver.
errors.custom_driver_too_large: The JAR exceeds the {{maxMb}} MB upload limit. Strip unused classes or split the bundle.
errors.custom_driver_validation: Some required upload fields are missing or invalid.
errors.custom_driver_generic: Could not upload the JDBC driver. Please try again.

editor.subtitle: Submit a query for AI and human review.
editor.justification_placeholder: Why are you running this query?

review_plan_preview.empty_description: This datasource has no review plan. Submit will go straight to execution policy.

ai_panel.title: AI analysis
ai_panel.empty_prompt_analyze: Click Analyze to run AI review on this query.

list.title: Query history
list.subtitle: All queries you've submitted or have access to view.
list.export_truncated: Export truncated at 50,000 rows. Apply tighter filters to capture the full result set.

detail.review_required_subtitle: Approve to forward to execution, reject to send back to submitter.
detail.review_request_changes_required: A comment is required when requesting changes.
detail.timeout_body_with_plan: Plan "{{plan}}" requires approval within {{hours}}h. Timed out {{when}}.
detail.timeout_body_without_plan: Approval window of {{hours}}h elapsed without a decision. Timed out {{when}}.

reviews.title: Review queue
reviews.subtitle: Queries awaiting your approval. Newest first.
reviews.empty_description: No queries are waiting on you right now.

list.title: Datasources
list.subtitle: Connected customer databases proxied through AccessFlow.
list.empty: No datasources yet — add one to get started.

create.title: Add datasource
create.subtitle: Connect a new customer database. AccessFlow will proxy and govern queries against it.
create.settings_step_subtitle: Configure limits and review policies for this datasource. You can change these later from the datasource settings page.
create.label_pool_size_help: Maximum number of concurrent JDBC connections AccessFlow keeps open to this datasource (1–200).
create.label_max_rows_help: Hard cap on rows returned for any single query routed through AccessFlow (1–1,000,000).
create.driver_status_bundled_hint: Driver ships inside AccessFlow. No network call is needed to use this database type.
create.driver_status_downloaded_hint: Driver has been downloaded from Maven Central and cached locally. Ready to use without network access.
create.driver_status_available_hint: Driver is not yet on disk. AccessFlow will download it from Maven Central on first use (typically 1–5 s) and cache it for next time.
create.driver_status_unavailable_hint: Driver cannot be obtained. Offline mode is enabled with an empty cache, or the cache directory is not writable. Check ACCESSFLOW_DRIVERS_OFFLINE and ACCESSFLOW_DRIVER_CACHE.
create.driver_policy_callout_body: AccessFlow bundles the PostgreSQL driver. Other database drivers are downloaded from Maven Central on first use, verified against pinned checksums, and cached locally for reuse.

type_description.postgresql: Open-source relational database. Driver bundled with AccessFlow.
type_description.mysql: Widely used open-source relational database from Oracle.
type_description.oracle: Oracle Database with the official driver.
type_description.mssql: Microsoft SQL Server with the official Microsoft driver.
type_description.custom: Free-form JDBC connection using an admin-uploaded driver.

create.source_uploaded_help: Admin-uploaded JDBC drivers for your organization. Manage them under Admin → Custom JDBC drivers.
create.no_custom_drivers_hint: No custom drivers uploaded yet — admins can add one to support new database types or driver versions.
create.field_jdbc_url_help: Full JDBC connection string passed verbatim to the uploaded driver.
create.dynamic_mode_notice_body: This database type uses the uploaded JDBC driver. Provide the complete JDBC URL — AccessFlow does not build it for you.
create.field_ai_config_required: Select an AI configuration or disable AI analysis
create.mysql_public_key_retrieval_body: MySQL 8+ uses caching_sha2_password by default and refuses to authenticate over a plain connection unless the client can fetch the server's RSA public key. AccessFlow adds allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true to the JDBC URL so login completes. The password is sent encrypted with that key, but the rest of the session is unencrypted. Choose REQUIRE or stronger to use TLS for the whole connection.
create.test_resolving_driver_hint: Downloading and verifying the driver. This only happens the first time.
create.test_failure_unknown: The connection could not be established. Check the credentials or network reachability.
create.test_step_subtitle: Run a one-shot SELECT 1 against the new datasource to confirm the driver loads and the credentials work.
create.types_error_body: The /datasources/types endpoint did not respond. Refresh the page or check your backend logs.

settings.delete_confirm_body: {{name}} will be marked inactive. Existing queries are preserved.
settings.ai_config_required: Pick an AI configuration or disable AI analysis
settings.permissions_subtitle: Granular per-user grants for this datasource. Admins automatically have full access.
settings.grant_user_required: Pick a user to grant access to.
settings.grant_row_limit_help: Optional. Caps the rows this user can fetch in a single query.
settings.grant_row_limit_min: Row limit must be at least 1.
settings.grant_schemas_help: Leave empty for full schema access. Press Enter after each schema name.
settings.grant_tables_help: Leave empty for full table access. Press Enter after each table name.
settings.grant_columns_help: Values in these columns are masked in query results. The AI reviewer is informed but does not auto-reject.
settings.grant_expires_help: Optional. Access auto-revokes after this date.
settings.grant_expires_future: Expiry must be in the future.
settings.grant_at_least_one_perm: Grant at least one of read, write, or DDL.
settings.grant_already_exists: This user already has a permission row for this datasource.
settings.grant_user_not_in_org: That user is not part of your organization.
settings.activity_empty: No queries have been submitted against this datasource yet.

setup_progress.title: Finish setting up AccessFlow
setup_progress.subtitle: Complete these steps to start reviewing queries.

users.title: Users
users.password_help: 8–128 characters. The user can change it after first login.
users.deactivate_confirm_body: {{email}} will be deactivated and signed out of all sessions.

audit.title: Audit log
audit.subtitle: Append-only, tamper-evident log of every action in the system.
audit.empty: No audit events match the current filter.

ai_configs.title: AI configurations
ai_configs.subtitle: Configure as many AI providers as you need. Each datasource binds to one.
ai_configs.empty_body: Add an AI configuration to enable risk analysis on a datasource.
ai_configs.delete_confirm_body: This will remove the configuration named {{name}}.
ai_configs.api_key_required: API key is required for this provider

wizard.title: New AI configuration
wizard.subtitle: Pick a provider, fill in the connection details, and run a test prompt.

notifications.title: Notification channels
notifications.subtitle: Where AccessFlow sends review events, approvals, and security alerts.
notifications.empty: No notification channels yet — add one to start receiving alerts.

oauth2.title: OAuth providers
oauth2.subtitle: Configure one or more OAuth 2.0 / OIDC providers so users can sign in with their existing accounts.

setup_intro.google: Open the <console>Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials</console>, create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID of type "Web application", and paste the redirect URI below into "Authorized redirect URIs".
setup_intro.github: Open <console>GitHub → Settings → Developer settings → OAuth Apps</console>, click "New OAuth App", and paste the redirect URI below into "Authorization callback URL".
setup_intro.microsoft: Open the <console>Microsoft Entra admin center → App registrations</console>, register a new application, and under Authentication → Redirect URIs add a Web platform entry with the URI below. Capture the tenant ID from the app's overview page.
setup_intro.gitlab: Open <console>GitLab → User Settings → Applications</console> (or Admin Area → Applications for an org-wide app), and paste the redirect URI below into "Redirect URI".

oauth2.callback_url_label: Redirect URI to register with the provider
oauth2.callback_url_help: Copy this exact URL into the provider's developer console. AccessFlow accepts the callback only at this path.
oauth2.label_client_secret_help: Leave the masked value untouched to keep the existing secret. Clear it to remove the secret.
oauth2.label_scopes_help: Space-separated. Leave empty to use the provider defaults.
oauth2.label_tenant_id_help: Use "common" for any Microsoft account, "organizations" for any work/school account, or a specific tenant GUID.

client_id_tooltip.google: The OAuth 2.0 Client ID created in the Google Cloud Console. Looks like 1234567890-abc.apps.googleusercontent.com.
client_id_tooltip.github: The GitHub OAuth App's Client ID (visible on the app's settings page once created).
client_id_tooltip.microsoft: The Application (client) ID shown on the app's Overview page in the Entra admin center.
client_id_tooltip.gitlab: The Application ID shown on the application's page after creation in GitLab.

client_secret_tooltip.google: Click "Create credentials" in Google Cloud and download the secret — it is only shown once.
client_secret_tooltip.github: Generate a new client secret on the GitHub OAuth App page. Copy it immediately; GitHub does not show it again.
client_secret_tooltip.microsoft: Under Certificates & secrets, create a new Client Secret. Copy the "Value" (not the Secret ID).
client_secret_tooltip.gitlab: Shown once on the application page right after creation under "Secret".

oauth2.scopes_tooltip: Space- or comma-separated. Leave empty to use the provider defaults ({{defaults}}).
oauth2.tenant_id_tooltip: Microsoft Entra tenant ID, GUID, or a well-known alias: "common" (any Microsoft account), "organizations" (any work or school account), or "consumers" (personal accounts only).
oauth2.delete_confirm: Remove this provider configuration? Users currently signed in stay signed in.

saml.title: SAML / SSO
saml.subtitle: Configure your identity provider for single sign-on.

languages.title: Languages
languages.subtitle: Pick which languages users can choose, the default for new accounts, and the language the AI analyzer responds in.
languages.section_user_languages_help: Users can pick their UI language from this list.
languages.section_ai_language_help: AI analysis summaries and suggestions are produced in this language for every query in the org. Risk levels stay in English.

review_plans.title: Review plans
review_plans.subtitle: Configurations that drive AI and human review for queries against datasources.
review_plans.empty: No review plans yet — add one to start governing query review.
review_plans.delete_confirm_body: {{name}} will be removed. This is permanent.
review_plans.validation_min_approvers: Add at least one approver when human approval is required.
review_plans.validation_user_or_role: Each approver row needs a user or a role.

events.REVIEW_TIMEOUT: Your query on {{datasource}} timed out awaiting review
events.AI_HIGH_RISK: AI flagged a high-risk query on {{datasource}}

profile.title: Profile settings
profile.subtitle: Manage your display name, password, and two-factor authentication.

display_name.title: Profile
display_name.subtitle: How your name appears across AccessFlow.
display_name.label: Display name

password.title: Password
password.subtitle: Choose a strong password you don't use elsewhere.
password.saved: Password updated. You'll be signed out of other sessions.
password.saml_disabled: Password is managed by your SAML identity provider.
password.oauth2_disabled: Password is managed by your OAuth provider. Update it in that provider's account settings.

totp.title: Two-factor authentication
totp.subtitle_disabled: Add a second step to sign-in using an authenticator app.
totp.subtitle_enabled: Two-factor authentication is enabled on this account.
totp.saml_disabled: Two-factor authentication is managed by your SAML identity provider.
totp.oauth2_disabled: Two-factor authentication is managed by your OAuth provider. Enable it in that provider's account settings.
totp.step_scan_help: Open Google Authenticator, 1Password, or any TOTP app and scan this code.
totp.step_verify_help: Confirm setup by entering the code your authenticator shows.
totp.backup_codes_help: Store these recovery codes somewhere safe. Each can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator.

api_keys.title: API keys
api_keys.description: Create personal API keys to authenticate the AccessFlow MCP server and other programmatic clients. Keys inherit your permissions and can be revoked at any time.
api_keys.name_size: Name must be between 1 and 100 characters.
api_keys.copy_once_warning: This is the only time the key is shown. Copy it now and store it somewhere safe — you won't be able to retrieve it again.
api_keys.empty: You haven't created any API keys yet.
api_keys.revoke_confirm: Revoke API key "{{name}}"? Clients using it will lose access immediately.

totp_login.title: Two-factor authentication
totp_login.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

list.title: Custom JDBC drivers
list.subtitle: Upload vendor-specific or in-house JDBC driver JARs for your organization. Datasources can then bind to these drivers instead of the bundled set.
list.empty_description: Upload a JDBC driver JAR to support forks, vendor builds, or database types beyond the bundled five.
list.delete_confirm_body: {{vendor}} ({{filename}}) will be removed. Any datasource that still references it must be deleted first.

upload.title: Upload JDBC driver
upload.subtitle: Select the driver JAR and enter the SHA-256 you computed locally. AccessFlow verifies the checksum before storing and loads the driver class to confirm it implements java.sql.Driver.
upload.target_db_type_help: Pick the matching bundled type to override its driver, or 'Custom / Dynamic' for a free-form JDBC connection.
upload.driver_class_help: Fully-qualified Java class name of the JDBC driver inside the JAR.
upload.sha256_help: Run `shasum -a 256 driver.jar` locally and paste the hex digest. AccessFlow rejects the upload if the bytes don't match.
upload.missing_jar: Select a JAR file before uploading.

validation.vendor_max: Vendor name must be at most 100 characters.
validation.driver_class_max: Driver class must be at most 255 characters.
validation.driver_class_pattern: Enter a fully-qualified Java class name (e.g. com.vendor.Driver).
